title: Koanophyllon wetmorei
intro: Koanophyllon wetmorei, also known as Wetmore's koanophyllon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon wetmorei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon wetmorei is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Mexico. Koanophyllon wetmorei belongs to the genus Koanophyllon, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as "tree daisies". These plants are known for their bright flowers and are often found in dry, woodland areas. Koanophyllon wetmorei is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage.
